cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what can be concluded from Isabelle Dinoire's remarks. The passage tells the story of Isabelle who was attacked by her dog and had a face transplant. The question asks what we can conclude from her remarks. Looking at the text, when Isabelle saw herself in the mirror for the first time after the operation, she said, "I was scared to look at myself, but when I did it was already marvelous and I couldn't believe it. I thought it would be blue and swollen but it was already beautiful." Also later she says, "I have been saved... It's a miracle somehow." Now, looking at the options: (A) she was too shocked to see herself in the mirror. But the text says she was scared but when she looked, it was "marvelous" and "beautiful." So she wasn't just shocked; she was positive about what she saw. So (A) might not be correct. (B) she was satisfied with the result of the operation. Her remarks indicate she was happy with the result, calling it "marvelous" and "beautiful," and feeling saved. This seems like a correct conclusion. (C) she thought her damaged face couldn't be repaired. The text says that doctors initially offered little hope with conventional surgery, but Isabelle didn't say she thought it couldn't be repaired. She was the patient, but the doctors suggested the transplant as an option. So (C) may not be correct based on her remarks. (D) she looked more beautiful than before. She said it was beautiful, but the text doesn't compare it to how she looked before the attack.
